results.title: Blackmagic PYXIS 12K
results.credit_line: Courtesy Blackmagic Design, Major Partner of ACMI
results.brief_description:
The Blackmagic PYXIS 12K is a compact box camera designed for flexibility. Made from precision CNC-machined aerospace aluminium, it is lightweight, strong and highly riggable. It can be mounted to drones, built into larger camera rigs, or adapted for documentary, commercial, feature film and personal projects.

Released in 2025, the PYXIS 12K brings high-resolution digital cinematography into a smaller, more customisable form. Its 12K sensor captures extremely detailed images, giving filmmakers more freedom to crop, stabilise or reframe footage. Its modular design allows filmmakers to shape the camera around each shoot, rather than working around a fixed camera body.

Its appeal lies in that combination: Hollywood-level resolution in a camera built for independent, professional and experimental workflows.

results.title: Shelly Belly Inna Real Life
results.headline_credit_for_label:
Single channel video

Duration: 23 minutes 52 seconds

Co-commissioned by The Vinyl Factory
results.headline_credit_for_label_zh_hans:
单频道影像
时长：23分52秒
由 The Vinyl Factory 共同委约
results.headline_credit_for_label_pa:
ਸਿੰਗਲ ਚੈਨਲ ਵੀਡੀਓ
ਅਵਧੀ: 23 ਮਿੰਟ 52 ਸਕਿੰਟ
The Vinyl Factory ਵੱਲੋਂ ਸਹਿ-ਕਮਿਸ਼ਨ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ
results.headline_credit_for_label_hi:
सिंगल चैनल वीडियो
अवधि: 23 मिनट 52 सेकंड
The Vinyl Factory द्वारा सह-आदेशित
results.description_override_zh_hans:
影片以一位舞者从古典芭蕾过渡到牙买加舞厅舞（dancehall）开场，一种风格缓缓转化为另一种。我们看到舞蹈如何跨越文化传播，并在人们的适应与重新诠释中发生变化，成为他们自己的表达。

受 The Vinyl Factory 委托，这件作品源于艺术家兼编舞家 Cecilia Bengolea 与牙买加金斯敦、西班牙镇和博格沃克的舞厅社区之间长期合作的发展。作品的核心人物是具有影响力的舞者 Shelly Belly，由她带领我们走进这个故事。

舞厅音乐（dancehall）于 20 世纪 70 年代末至 80 年代在牙买加兴起，作为一种节奏更快、电子化程度更高的雷鬼音乐风格。它在面临贫困和政治紧张局势的社区中发展起来，街头舞会成为人们聚集的场所。人们来到这里被看见、竞逐并表达自我，围绕着强大的音响系统，在厚重的低音与闷热中持续舞动数小时。这些聚会成为共同释放的空间，由音乐、动作与社群共同塑造。

*注意：本作品包含一些粗俗语言*
results.description_override_pa:
ਫ਼ਿਲਮ ਦੀ ਸ਼ੁਰੂਆਤ ਇੱਕ ਨਰਤਕੀ ਨਾਲ ਹੁੰਦੀ ਹੈ ਜੋ ਕਲਾਸੀਕਲ ਬੈਲੇ ਤੋਂ ਡਾਂਸਹਾਲ ਵੱਲ ਵਧਦੀ ਹੈ, ਜਿਵੇਂ ਇੱਕ ਅੰਦਾਜ਼ ਹੌਲੇ-ਹੌਲੇ ਦੂਜੇ ਵਿੱਚ ਬਦਲ ਜਾਂਦਾ ਹੈ। ਅਸੀਂ ਵੇਖਦੇ ਹਾਂ ਕਿ ਨਾਚ ਕਿਵੇਂ ਸਭਿਆਚਾਰਾਂ ਪਾਰ ਯਾਤਰਾ ਕਰਦਾ ਹੈ, ਅਤੇ ਲੋਕ ਉਸਨੂੰ ਆਪਣੇ ਅਨੁਸਾਰ ਢਾਲ ਕੇ ਆਪਣਾ ਬਣਾਉਂਦੇ ਹੋਏ ਕਿਵੇਂ ਬਦਲ ਦਿੰਦੇ ਹਨ।

The Vinyl Factory ਵੱਲੋਂ ਕਮਿਸ਼ਨ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ ਇਹ ਰਚਨਾ ਕਲਾਕਾਰ ਅਤੇ ਕੋਰੀਓਗ੍ਰਾਫਰ Cecilia Bengolea ਅਤੇ ਜਮਾਇਕਾ ਦੇ Kingston, Spanish Town ਅਤੇ Bog Walk ਦੀਆਂ ਡਾਂਸਹਾਲ ਕਮਿਊਨਿਟੀਆਂ ਨਾਲ ਲੰਬੇ ਸਹਿਯੋਗ ਤੋਂ ਵਿਕਸਿਤ ਹੋਈ। ਇਸ ਦੇ ਕੇਂਦਰ ਵਿੱਚ Shelly Belly ਹੈ, ਇੱਕ ਪ੍ਰਭਾਵਸ਼ਾਲੀ ਨਰਤਕ, ਜੋ ਸਾਨੂੰ ਇਸ ਕਹਾਣੀ ਰਾਹੀਂ ਲੰਘਾਉਂਦਾ ਹੈ।

ਡਾਂਸਹਾਲ ਦੀ ਸ਼ੁਰੂਆਤ ਜਮਾਇਕਾ ਵਿੱਚ 1970 ਦੇ ਆਖ਼ਰੀ ਦਹਾਕੇ ਅਤੇ 1980 ਦੇ ਦਹਾਕੇ ਵਿੱਚ ਰੇਗੇ ਦੇ ਇੱਕ ਤੇਜ਼ਤਰ, ਹੋਰ ਇਲੈਕਟ੍ਰਾਨਿਕ ਅੰਦਾਜ਼ ਵਜੋਂ ਹੋਈ। ਇਹ ਉਹਨਾਂ ਪੜੋਸਾਂ ਵਿੱਚ ਫੈਲਿਆ ਜਿੱਥੇ ਗਰੀਬੀ ਅਤੇ ਰਾਜਨੀਤਿਕ ਤਣਾਅ ਸੀ, ਅਤੇ ਜਿੱਥੇ ਗਲੀ-ਮੁਹੱਲਿਆਂ ਦੇ ਨਾਚ-ਸਮਾਗਮ ਇਕੱਠੇ ਹੋਣ ਦੇ ਥਾਂ ਬਣ ਗਏ। ਲੋਕ ਉੱਥੇ ਵੇਖੇ ਜਾਣ, ਮੁਕਾਬਲਾ ਕਰਨ ਅਤੇ ਆਪਣੇ ਆਪ ਨੂੰ ਪ੍ਰਗਟ ਕਰਨ ਲਈ ਆਉਂਦੇ ਸਨ, ਤਾਕਤਵਰ ਸਾਊਂਡ ਸਿਸਟਮਾਂ ਦੇ ਗੇੜੇ ਪਾ ਕੇ, ਭਾਰੀ ਬੇਸ ਅਤੇ ਗਰਮੀ ਵਿੱਚ ਘੰਟਿਆਂ ਤੱਕ ਨੱਚਦੇ ਸਨ। ਇਹ ਇਕੱਠ ਸਾਂਝੀ ਰਿਹਾਈ ਦੇ ਸਥਾਨ ਬਣ ਗਏ, ਜਿਨ੍ਹਾਂ ਨੂੰ ਸੰਗੀਤ, ਹਿਲਚਲ ਅਤੇ ਕਮਿਊਨਿਟੀ ਨੇ ਆਕਾਰ ਦਿੱਤਾ।

*ਨੋਟ: ਇਸ ਰਚਨਾ ਵਿੱਚ ਕੁਝ ਅਸ਼ਲੀਲ ਭਾਸ਼ਾ ਸ਼ਾਮਲ ਹੈ*
results.description_override_hi:
फिल्म की शुरुआत एक नर्तकी से होती है जो शास्त्रीय बैले से डांसहॉल की ओर बढ़ती है, जहाँ एक शैली धीरे-धीरे दूसरी में बदल जाती है। हम देखते हैं कि नृत्य कैसे संस्कृतियों के पार यात्रा करता है, और लोग उसे अपनाकर और अपना बनाकर कैसे बदल देते हैं।

The Vinyl Factory के लिए कमीशन किया गया यह कार्य कलाकार और कोरियोग्राफर Cecilia Bengolea तथा जमैका के Kingston, Spanish Town और Bog Walk की डांसहॉल समुदायों के साथ लंबे सहयोग से विकसित हुआ। इसके केंद्र में Shelly Belly हैं, एक प्रभावशाली नर्तक, जो हमें इस कहानी के माध्यम से ले जाते हैं।

डांसहॉल की शुरुआत जमैका में 1970 के दशक के उत्तरार्ध और 1980 के दशक में रेगे की एक अधिक तेज़, अधिक इलेक्ट्रॉनिक शैली के रूप में हुई। यह उन इलाकों में विकसित हुआ जहाँ गरीबी और राजनीतिक तनाव था, और जहाँ सड़क पर होने वाले नृत्य-समारोह लोगों के इकट्ठा होने की जगह बन गए। लोग वहाँ दिखने, प्रतिस्पर्धा करने और स्वयं को व्यक्त करने आते थे, शक्तिशाली साउंड सिस्टमों के चारों ओर घिरकर, भारी बेस और गर्मी के बीच घंटों तक थिरकते हुए। ये जुटान साझा मुक्तिविसर्जन के स्थल बन गए, जिन्हें संगीत, गति और समुदाय ने आकार दिया।

*नोट: इस कार्य में कुछ अशिष्ट भाषा शामिल है*
results.description:
The film begins with a dancer moving from classical ballet into dancehall, as one style slowly transforms into another. We see how dance travels across cultures, changing as people adapt it and make it their own.

Commissioned by The Vinyl Factory, the work grew from a long collaboration between artist and choreographer Cecilia Bengolea and dancehall communities in Kingston, Spanish Town and Bog Walk in Jamaica. At its centre is Shelly Belly, an influential dancer who guides us through the story.

Dancehall began in Jamaica in the late 1970s and 80s as a faster, more electronic style of reggae. It grew in neighbourhoods facing poverty and political tension, where street dances became places to gather. People came to be seen, compete and express themselves, circling powerful sound systems and moving for hours to heavy bass and heat. These gatherings became spaces of shared release, shaped by music, movement and community.

Note: this work contains some coarse language

Commissioned by The Vinyl Factory, the work grew from a long collaboration between artist and choreographer Cecilia Bengolea and dancehall communities in Kingston, Spanish Town and Bog Walk in Jamaica. At its centre is Shelly Belly, an influential dancer who guides us through the story.

Dancehall began in Jamaica in the late 1970s and 80s as a faster, more electronic style of reggae. It grew in neighbourhoods facing poverty and political tension, where street dances became places to gather. People came to be seen, compete and express themselves, circling powerful sound systems and moving for hours to heavy bass and heat. results.title_override_zh_hans: 现实生活中的 Shelly Belly

results.title: [KenRa Films Present: the Kenneth Rankine Home Movie Collection: Reel 19]
results.brief_description:
Kenneth Carruthers Rankine (1890 - 1968) was a keen amateur cinematographer and filmmaker. This collection of his home movies has been donated to ACMI by Kenneth’s granddaughter. The collection represents a wonderful slice of life in Australia; with contents including scenes of everyday life, major family occasions, holidays around Victoria and views of Melbourne.

This film shows views across the Melbourne CBD rooftops and steeples, a selection of ‘flowering gums’, a visit to the Botanical Gardens, the arrival of ‘flat tops’ aircraft carrier ships. Kenneth then shows off the neon signs of Melbourne at night.

Selected highlights list:

00:00:20:00 title ‘Melbourne’.

00:00:26:00 views from what appears to be the top of the Royal Melbourne Children’s Hospital in North Melbourne with a note, ‘From Here 178 Feet’ beginning with a view down Elizabeth St toward the CBD.

00:01:06:00 an eastern view, where the Dandenong Ranges can be seen in the distance.

00:01:28:00 to 00:01:37:00 views over Melbourne University toward Queen’s College and the Royal Exhibition Building.

00:01:58:00 a horse and cart enter a city intersection at same time as a tram and cars.

00:02:47:00 Melbourne Technical College rotunda.

00:03:05:00 the Pelaco sign in Richmond (a Heritage Victoria listed landmark). Pelaco is a Melbourne based shirt manufacturing business.

00:30:00:00 Elizabeth St with Flinders St Station in view ends the footage of the rooftops of Melbourne.

00:04:30:00 title card ‘Flowering Gums’ followed by a selection of trees and shrubs in flower.

00:06:52:00 title card ‘Botanical Gardens’ begins a look at people enjoying the gardens. Footage includes examples of fox furs being worn by some women strolling in the gardens.

00:09:28:00 a quick look at what appears to be Captain Cook’s cottage.

00:09:35:00 title card ‘V.A.C.S On Location’ begins a sequence of what appears to be a film being made.

00:10:45:00 title card ‘The Flat Tops Arrive’ footage of large ships (aircraft carriers) and tugboats in the bay viewed by watchers on a small pier near, presumably, Port Melbourne.

00:12:42:00 a plane goes overhead. The sequence ends at at night on the beach. with people overlooking the bay toward the horizon.

00:15:11:00 title ‘Bright Lights’, a sequence at night showing neon signs around Melbourne.

00:16:03:00 the face of Luna Park in St Kilda.

00:16:24:00 Fostor’s Lager advertising sign.

00:16:35:00 Berger Paints (British Paints) advertising sign.

00:16:40:00 to 00:16:55:00 Swallows and Ariells ‘Follow the Swallow’ sequenced advertisement for Uneeda Biscuits, Plum Pudding and Biscuits Cakes Ice cream.

00:16:58:00 Sunshine Milk advertising sign.

00:17:15:00 Ball and Welch department store sign on Flinders St with the T&G Building sign in the background.

00:17:23:00 shows a longer sequence advertisement for Sennets ice cream featuring a bear eating an ice cream.

00:18:00:00 possibly the top dome of the Royal Exhibition Building lit up.

00:18:25:00 ‘Visit the Zoo’ advertising flashes up on screen.

00:19:05:00 advertisement ‘Mascot Underwear for Men and Boys’ featuring a ram and lamb on his back.

00:19:09:00 ‘Dunlop Tyres’ advertising sign.

00:19:33:00 footage ends with blurred city lights and traffic lights turning to red.

results.title: KenRa Films Presents: Join the Vic. Sporting Car Club Now
results.brief_description:
In this silent film, Ken Rankine takes viewers to the sidelines at some of Victoria’s historic sports car races in 1954 and 1955. Tracks featured include the Templestowe Hill Climb, Rob Roy Hillclimb and the Altona Circuit.

Kenneth Carruthers Rankine (1890 - 1968) was a keen amateur cinematographer and filmmaker. This collection of his home movies has been donated to ACMI by Kenneth’s granddaughter. The collection represents a wonderful slice of life in Australia; with contents including scenes of everyday life, major family occasions, holidays around Victoria and views of Melbourne.

results.title: The Cute Machine
results.creator_credit: Martine Corompt and Australian Film Commission
results.brief_description:
The Cute Machine is an exploration into the morphology of cute characterisation and iconography and its relationship to the biological world, seeking to analyse this ‘Cute ideal’ by correlating it with the Zoological process known as Neoteny

The CD contains two sections that had already been exhibited as installations (Sorry! and Activity Station) but were then adapted to a screen version. The remaining sections elaborate on the themes of cute iconography and its analysis.

The Cute Machine proposes an environment where cute iconography and user friendliness have rampantly accelerated, and reduced computer interface design to the look and sophistication of an infant toy, while also taking the premise, Cute = young to the extreme, where the ‘Cutest’, becomes an abstracted mutation of the original sentiment.

constellations_primary.name: From cute to evil
constellations_primary.description: In The Cute Machine, players are rewarded by making characters with the most juvenile or baby-ish features. Explore how broader screen works uphold or disrupt the idea that characters with cute traits are inherently good and those without are evil.

results.description:
Artistic videogames are becoming common, but they’re not a new phenomenon. In the 1990s, Australian artist Martine Corompt made videogame artworks that blended biological theory with technological development to explore the concept of cuteness. One aspect that Corompt investigated is ‘neoteny’, a term that describes how some species retain juvenile, ‘cute’ traits in adulthood to make others feel protective of them.

She drew a parallel between neoteny and the changing aesthetic of tech in the 90s. Her early artworks were made on Amiga, but she noted that Apple computers began introducing faces to their interface in the 90s, and messages addressing the user directly, creating a sense of familiarity — the computer wasn’t just a tool, but a friend.

The Cute Machine morphs and mutates ‘cuteness’ to the extreme, blurring the boundaries between innocence and transgression.

Please be advised, this work contains cartoon violence, nudity and adult themes.
description_for_label:
The Cute Machine is an exploration into the morphology of cute characterisation and iconography and its relationship to the biological world, seeking to analyse this ‘Cute ideal’ by correlating it with the Zoological process known as Neoteny

The CD contains two sections that had already been exhibited as installations (Sorry! and Activity Station) but were then adapted to a screen version. The remaining sections elaborate on the themes of cute iconography and its analysis.

The Cute Machine proposes an environment where cute iconography and user friendliness have rampantly accelerated, and reduced computer interface design to the look and sophistication of an infant toy, while also taking the premise, Cute = young to the extreme, where the ‘Cutest’, becomes an abstracted mutation of the original sentiment.

results.title: Bieberchella
results.material_description: digital screen recording of @faithhtenorioo’s TikTok stream
results.brief_description: This performance turns Justin Bieber’s screen into a portrait of the early internet. As he sings with his younger self, memes like Double Rainbow and Deez Nuts do more than add humour. They evoke the online world that produced him: a culture of webcam confession, viral randomness and participatory watching. Double Rainbow captures the raw sincerity of early YouTube, while Deez Nuts represents its throwaway, repeatable joke logic. Bieber belongs to both. He was one of the first pop stars built through that ecosystem, where intimacy and exposure fed each other. By folding memes into the set, he reframes his career as inseparable from the internet’s gaze.

Bieber belongs to both. He was one of the first pop stars built through that ecosystem, where intimacy and exposure fed each other. By folding memes into the set, he reframes his career as inseparable from the internet’s gaze.

results.title: Flesh on glass
results.creator_credit: Ann Turner, Lucy MacLaren and Swinburne Institute of Technology. Film and Television School
results.brief_description: Kate’s love for her brother’s wife and the ensuing feelings of guilt lead her to an obsession with the supernatural. We follow her struggle towards self-awareness.

results.title: Le Voyage dans la Lune (A Trip to the Moon)
results.brief_description: Melies’ fantastical vision of life on the moon.

constellations_other.name: Space, sci-fi and cinema fakes
constellations_other.description: Magic lantern slides were used to entertain and educate audiences in home, theatres and universities. While performers told jokes and fairytales, travelling showmen unveiled exotic locations and scientists revealed the latest discoveries.

results.title_for_label: Le Voyage dans la Lune (A Trip to the Moon)
results.description: One of the first Science Fiction films, this Melies’ short humorously proposes what man discovers when he first lands on the moon. Based on the Jules Verne story, George Melies used his extraordinary cinematic illusions to tell this story of the first voyage to the moon. Made in 1902, this was a visionary film that has come to be recognised as a cinematic masterpiece. Among the many comical set pieces is the famous scene in which a rocket ship crash-lands into the eye of the ‘man in the moon’. This whimsical fantasy is also exemplary in its early depiction of the moon and the possible encounters between humans and worlds beyond our imagining, in our exploration of deep space.
description_for_label: One of the first Science Fiction films, this Melies’ short humorously proposes what man discovers when he first lands on the moon. results.title: [Cinesound Review 1950-1965]
results.brief_description:
This film contains excerpts from Cinesound Review, a news broadcast that covered significant social, cultural and political events in Australia. The film features a number of different clips from newsreels between 1950 and 1965.

The first section, titled ‘1950 Melbourne Cup’, shows the horse race at Flemington Racecourse, photographed by Ron Horner. The film includes close-ups of people in the crowd, and commentary of the race as Comic Court wins. This is followed by a section titled ‘Scone Rodeo: Bronchos (sic) on the Bust’, featuring footage of a horse rodeo in Scone, New South Wales.

The third section, titled ‘Fur Flies: Battling Budgerigar K.O.’s Cat’ features a playfight between two pets, a ginger cat and a budgerigar. In the fourth section we see ANZAC Day parades in Melbourne and Sydney, preluded by the title ‘ANZAC: Nation’s Homage’.

The fifth section is titled ‘Skiing: Day and Night Fun at Mt. Buller’, with commentary by David Low. It features children learning how to ski and social activities in the resorts, including bands and dancing. This is followed by an excerpt called ‘Clark Well Ahead’, showing the motorcar race at the 1965 British Grand Prix, won by Jim Clark.

The film ends with a cartoon called ‘Local Boy Makes Good: A Story with a Moral’ and three feature film trailers: ‘The Counterfeit Traitor’ (1962), ‘Pollyanna’ (1960) and ‘Mein Kampf’ (1960).

results.title: [John Anderson Home Movies: Reel 25]
results.brief_description:
John Robert (Jack) Anderson (1924–1993) was a film distributor, cinema owner and photographer as well as an enthusiastic filmmaker in and around Ballarat in Victoria. Jack developed an interest in film very early at cinema shows and gained his first film experience while serving in WWII, photographing wartime conditions and aircraft involved in the defence of Australia’s north, including the aftermath of the Darwin bombing raid.

It was whilst working in photographic studios that Jack met his future wife, business partner and fellow film enthusiast Marie Bourke. Starting out by hiring films and showing them around regional Victoria, Jack and Marie established cinemas such as the Astor Cinema in Ararat, Bell Post Hill Drive-In in Geelong and the Southern and Northern Drive-Ins and Vegas 70 Cinema in Ballarat. In the 1970s, the Anderson family acquired and restored the Regent Theatre in Ballarat which would then become the first multiplex in regional Victoria.

Along the way, Jack filmed his family and friends activities and outings, horse racing, Ballarat street scenes, motorcycle scrambling and travel scenes on the road – an unusual and wonderful look at life in regional Victoria during the 1940s to the 1960s.

This film presents scenes from suburban family life in Ballarat and holiday footage from the Mornington Peninsula.

This film was originally made using 16mm film.

Selected highlights list:
00:03:05:00 a child falling over as he kicks a football in a field
00:05:54:00 two children throw a fully clothed person into a swimming pool
00:08:35:00 slow-motion of three children running and jumping into a pool together
00:19:40:00 footage of child water skiing with Sorrento foreshore and the Continental Hotel in the background
